sections_cited: Section 25 of the Sales Tax Act

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P.No.D-2680 of 2024 --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE(S) OF JUDGE(S) ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 1.For hearing of CMA No.12238/2024 2.For hearing of main case. 13.05.2025 Mr.Hanif Faisal Alam, Advocate for the Petitioner Ms.Alizeh Bashir, Assistant Attorney General. Mr.Muhammad Akbar Khan, Assistant Attorney General. Mr,Munawar Ali Memon, Advocate for Respondent. On the last date following order was passed:- “09.05.2025 Mr. Khalid Jawed Khan, advocate for the petitioner Ms. Alizeh Bashir, Assistant Attorney General Mr. Muhammad Akbar Khan, Assistant Attorney General Mr. Abdul Hafeez, Commissioner Zone-VI, LTO, Karachi Mr. Munawar Ali Memon advocate files Vakalatnama on behalf of respondent which is taken on record. Learned commissioner is present and states that the main grievance of the petitioner is that the notice under Section 25 of the Sales Tax Act, 1990, was issued prior to date for compliance given in the notice under Section 25 of Sales Tax Act, 1990. He states that the department is willing to withdraw the notice under Section 25 of Sales Tax Act, 1990 and any subsequent notice in pursuance thereof, and the petition may be disposed of in the said terms. Learned counsel for the petitioner seeks time to obtain instructions. At his request, to come up on 13.05.2025. Interim order passed earlier to continue till the next date of hearing. The presence and assistance of the learned Commissioner is appreciated and dispensed with.” Today learned counsel for the petitioner submits upon instructions that the petitioner would be satisfied if all subsequent notices pursuant to the initial notice per Section 25 were withdrawn.

Interim order passed earlier to continue till the next date of hearing. The presence and assistance of the learned Commissioner is appreciated and dispensed with.” Today learned counsel for the petitioner submits upon instructions that the petitioner would be satisfied if all subsequent notices pursuant to the initial notice per Section 25 were withdrawn. He also seeks permission to satisfy the respondents of the initial notice within 14 days hereof. Learned counsel for the respondent states that as represented in the earlier order the Department shall withdraw all subsequent notices subject to right of the Department to initiate and pursue any action as may be permissible under the law. It is jointly stated that this petition may be disposed of in the said terms. Order accordingly. Judge Judge nasir
